---
title: Importe
jupyter: python3
---



```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
```

# Load Data

```{python}
account = pd.read_csv("../data/raw/account.csv", sep=";")
card = pd.read_csv("../data/raw/card.csv", sep=";")
client = pd.read_csv("../data/raw/client.csv", sep=";")
disp = pd.read_csv("../data/raw/disp.csv", sep=";")
district = pd.read_csv("../data/raw/district.csv", sep=";")
loan = pd.read_csv("../data/raw/loan.csv", sep=";")
order = pd.read_csv("../data/raw/order.csv", sep=";")
trans = pd.read_csv("../data/raw/trans.csv", sep=";", low_memory=False)
```

# EDA
## PK Check

### Account

```{python}
print("Unique:", account["account_id"].is_unique)
print("Count:", account.shape[0])
print("Duplicates:", account["account_id"].duplicated().sum())
print("Missing values:", account["account_id"].isna().sum())

print("\nData type of account_id:", account["account_id"].dtype)
print("All same type:", account["account_id"].apply(type).nunique() == 1)
```

### Card / Credit Card

```{python}
print("Unique:", card["disp_id"].is_unique)
print("Count:", card.shape[0])
print("Duplicates:", card["disp_id"].duplicated().sum())
print("Missing values:", card["disp_id"].isna().sum())

print("\nData type of disp_id:", card["disp_id"].dtype)
print("All same type:", card["disp_id"].apply(type).nunique() == 1)
```

### Client

```{python}
print("Unique:", client["client_id"].is_unique)
print("Count:", client.shape[0])
print("Duplicates:", client["client_id"].duplicated().sum())
print("Missing values:", client["client_id"].isna().sum())

print("\nData type of client_id:", client["client_id"].dtype)
print("All same type:", client["client_id"].apply(type).nunique() == 1)
```

### Disp / Dispostion

```{python}
print("Unique:", disp["disp_id"].is_unique)
print("Count:", disp.shape[0])
print("Duplicates:", disp["disp_id"].duplicated().sum())
print("Missing values:", disp["disp_id"].isna().sum())

print("\nData type of disp_id:", disp["disp_id"].dtype)
print("All same type:", disp["disp_id"].apply(type).nunique() == 1)
```

### District / Demograph

```{python}
print("Unique:", district["A1"].is_unique)
print("Count:", district.shape[0])
print("Duplicates:", district["A1"].duplicated().sum())
print("Missing values:", district["A1"].isna().sum())

print("\nData type of A1:", district["A1"].dtype)
print("All same type:", district["A1"].apply(type).nunique() == 1)
```

### Loan

```{python}
print("Unique:", loan["loan_id"].is_unique)
print("Count:", loan.shape[0])
print("Duplicates:", loan["loan_id"].duplicated().sum())
print("Missing values:", loan["loan_id"].isna().sum())

print("\nData type of loan_id:", loan["loan_id"].dtype)
print("All same type:", loan["loan_id"].apply(type).nunique() == 1)
```

### Order / Permanent Order

```{python}
print("Unique:", order["order_id"].is_unique)
print("Count:", order.shape[0])
print("Duplicates:", order["order_id"].duplicated().sum())
print("Missing values:", order["order_id"].isna().sum())

print("\nData type of order_id:", order["order_id"].dtype)
print("All same type:", order["order_id"].apply(type).nunique() == 1)
```

### Trans / Transactions

```{python}
print("Unique:", trans["trans_id"].is_unique)
print("Count:", trans.shape[0])
print("Duplicates:", trans["trans_id"].duplicated().sum())
print("Missing values:", trans["trans_id"].isna().sum())

print("\nData type of trans_id:", trans["trans_id"].dtype)
print("All same type:", trans["trans_id"].apply(type).nunique() == 1)
```

### Entschlüsse
- Alle Counts stimmen mit dem pdf überein
- Alle PK sind Unique
- Alle PK sind vorhanden
- Alle PK haben denselben Typ

> Bei district / Demographic ist die PK Spalte nicht ```district_id``` sonder ```A1```. Diese Spalte wird von ```A1``` nach ```district_id``` umbenannt. 

```{python}
district.rename(columns={"A1": "district_id"}, inplace=True)
district.head()
```

## Kardinalitäten Check

```{python}
# Define all FK relationships: (fk_table, fk_col, pk_table, pk_col)
relations = [
    (
        account,
        "district_id",
        district,
        "district_id",
        "account.district_id -> district.district_id",
    ),
    (
        client,
        "district_id",
        district,
        "district_id",
        "client.district_id -> district.district_id",
    ),
    (disp, "client_id", client, "client_id", "disp.client_id -> client.client_id"),
    (
        disp,
        "account_id",
        account,
        "account_id",
        "disp.account_id -> account.account_id",
    ),
    (card, "disp_id", disp, "disp_id", "card.disp_id -> disp.disp_id"),
    (
        loan,
        "account_id",
        account,
        "account_id",
        "loan.account_id -> account.account_id",
    ),
    (
        order,
        "account_id",
        account,
        "account_id",
        "order.account_id -> account.account_id",
    ),
    (
        trans,
        "account_id",
        account,
        "account_id",
        "trans.account_id -> account.account_id",
    ),
]

rows = []
for fk_df, fk_col, pk_df, pk_col, label in relations:
    fk_values = fk_df[fk_col].dropna()
    pk_values = pk_df[pk_col]

    # Referential integrity: all FK values exist in PK?
    orphans = ~fk_values.isin(pk_values)

    # Cardinality: how many FK rows per PK value?
    counts = fk_values.value_counts()

    rows.append(
        {
            "Relation": label,
            "FK rows": len(fk_values),
            "FK nulls": fk_df[fk_col].isna().sum(),
            "Orphans": orphans.sum(),
            "PK used": fk_values.nunique(),
            "PK total": len(pk_values),
            "Min": counts.min(),
            "Max": counts.max(),
            "Median": counts.median(),
            "Cardinality": "1:1" if counts.max() == 1 else f"1:N (max {counts.max()})",
        }
    )

card_df = pd.DataFrame(rows)
card_df.style.set_properties(**{"text-align": "left"})
```

### Erkenntnisse

- Keine Orphans und keine FK-Nulls -> referentielle Integrität ist vollständig gegeben
- **disp** ist die zentrale Brücken-Tabelle zwischen Client und Account

| Relation | Kardinalität |
|---|---|
| district -> account | 1:N (32–554) |
| district -> client | 1:N (43–663) |
| client -> disp | 1:1 |
| account -> disp | 1:N (max 2) |
| disp -> card | 1:1 (optional, 892/5369) |
| account -> loan | 1:1 (optional, 682/4500) |
| account -> order | 1:N (max 5) |
| account -> trans | 1:N (max 675) |


> Da `card.disp_id` als PK der Card-Tabelle verwendet wird und gleichzeitig FK auf disp ist, ist die Beziehung disp -> card tatsächlich **1:0..1** (optional 1:1).

## ERD
**Info:** Aktuell fehlen noch die Werte ob ein Attribut unique ist und ob es optional ist oder nicht
![title](../erd/aml.png)


```{python}
trans.info()
```
